title: ReDeBug: Finding Unpatched Code Clones in Entire OS Distributions

evidence_units.text_anchor: # Abstract Abstract—Programmers should never ﬁx the same bug twice. Unfortunately this often happens when patches to buggy code are not propagated to all code clones. Unpatched code clones represent latent bugs, and for
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # I. INTRODUCTION Patches to buggy code are often not propagated to code clones in real OS distributions. For example, the following patch was issued for Expat, a widely used XML parser to ﬁx a bounds checking bug in Aug
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # II. REDEBUG A. The Core System Finding all unpatched code clones is tricky and involves numerous considerations. For example, how many lines of code need to be similar for a case to be reported? Is one copied line enou
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # III. IMPLEMENTATION & EVALUATION A. Implementation ReDeBug is implemented in about 1000 lines of C code and 250 lines of Python. Normalization is mod- ularized within the Python code. We use the QuickLZ library [3] to
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # IV. DISCUSSION A. Comparison to Prior Work ReDeBug improves scalability with decreased false de- tection rate, but may ﬁnd fewer code clones than previous code clone detection work. In order to measure the number of un
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # V. RELATED WORK MOSS [28] is a well-known similarity detection tool using n-tokens. MOSS is based upon an algorithm called winnowing [28], a fuzzy hashing technique that selects a subset of n-tokens to ﬁnd similar code
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.

evidence_units.text_anchor: # VI. CONCLUSION In this paper we presented ReDeBug, an architecture designed for unpatched code clone detection. ReDeBug was designed for scalability to entire OS distributions, the ability to handle real code, and mini
evidence_units.notes: Initial agent index generated from section_text.
